gene_description: Beta-2 Adrenergic Receptor — target of beta-2 agonists in asthma and COPD. Arg16Gly polymorphism affects receptor regulation and bronchodilator responsiveness.

source.title: ADRB2 polymorphism and salbutamol responsiveness in Northern Indian children with mild to moderate exacerbation of asthma
source.finding: ADRB2 rs1042713 Arg16Gly polymorphism shows variable association with salbutamol bronchodilator response across populations. Meta-analyses show inconsistent effects on FEV1 improvement post-inhalation.

AA.effect: Homozygous for Arg16. Sustained beta-2 receptor function with repeated beta-agonist use. Better long-term bronchodilator response with salbutamol and other short-acting beta agonists. Lower risk of receptor desensitization. Represents ~42-45% of Europeans.
AA.recommendation: Standard bronchodilator dosing. Good response expected with short-acting beta-2 agonists. May show improved long-term control. LABA use as add-on controller therapy generally well-tolerated.

AG.effect: Mixed Arg16/Gly16 alleles. Intermediate beta-2 receptor regulation. Variable bronchodilator response to inhaled beta-agonists. Moderate desensitization risk with chronic LABA use. Associated with increased asthma exacerbations in some pediatric cohorts using LABA as add-on controller.
AG.recommendation: Standard bronchodilator dosing with careful monitoring. Assess inhaler technique and asthma control regularly. Monitor for loss of bronchodilator efficacy over time. Consider LABA formulation choice and monitoring intensity.

GA.effect: Mixed Arg16/Gly16 alleles. Intermediate beta-2 receptor regulation. Variable bronchodilator response to inhaled beta-agonists. Moderate desensitization risk with chronic LABA use. Associated with increased asthma exacerbations in some pediatric cohorts using LABA as add-on controller.
GA.recommendation: Standard bronchodilator dosing with careful monitoring. Assess inhaler technique and asthma control regularly. Monitor for loss of bronchodilator efficacy over time. Consider LABA formulation choice and monitoring intensity.

GG.effect: Homozygous for Gly16. Increased beta-2 receptor desensitization, particularly with chronic beta-agonist use. Reduced long-term bronchodilator efficacy. Higher risk of loss of response to LABA therapy. Associated with increased nocturnal asthma symptoms despite LABA use in some studies.
GG.recommendation: Monitor bronchodilator response closely — may show reduced efficacy over time. Consider leukotriene inhibitors or inhaled corticosteroid optimization as primary controller. LABA as add-on therapy may be less effective; ensure adequate ICS dosing. Regular asthma control assessment essential.
